q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is HttpResponse(body, content_type='text/html') which constructs an HTTP response object, not a dangerous side-effect in a test environment.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The XSS violation (unescaped user input in HTML) is embedded in the returned HttpResponse body, which can be inspected after the function returns.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' with expr 'HttpResponse(body, content_type='text/html')', meaning the response object with the tainted body is directly accessible in memory on return.
